senses.definition: a building's main structural component, such as its walls, roof, etc.
a building, usually next or attached to a house, in which cars or other vehicles are kept

examples.x: They parked their car in the garage to protect it from the harsh winter weather.
The garage was filled with tools and equipment for home improvement projects.
He decided to convert the garage into a small workshop for his woodworking hobby.
After cleaning out the garage, they found an old bicycle that had been forgotten for years.

senses.definition: a specialized storage space designed to store and age wine bottles under controlled temperature, humidity, and lighting conditions to preserve their quality and flavor over time

examples.x: The old mansion had a vast wine cellar that had been in use for centuries.
She invited friends over for a wine-tasting event in her beautifully designed wine cellar.
They converted the unused space under the stairs into a small wine cellar to store their growing collection.
He spent hours organizing his collection in the wine cellar, labeling each bottle carefully.
They installed a wine cellar in the basement, adding both style and functionality to the house.

senses.definition: an underground storage space or room, typically found in a building, used for storing food, wine, or other items that require a cool and dark environment

examples.x: They use the cellar to store homemade preserves and pickles.
The cellar was filled with dusty bottles and old wooden crates.
She found a hidden door in the kitchen that led down to the cellar.
He installed a temperature control system in the cellar to keep his wine collection at the perfect temperature.
The old cellar had thick stone walls that kept it cool even in the summer.

senses.definition: the front of a building, particularly one that is large and has an elegant appearance

examples.x: The facade of the historic building was adorned with intricate carvings and ornate details, showcasing the craftsmanship of its era.
The modern skyscraper had a sleek glass facade, reflecting the surrounding cityscape and creating a striking visual impression.
The theater's facade featured a marquee sign and grand entrance, inviting patrons to experience the magic of live performances within.
The colonial-style mansion had a white-painted facade with elegant columns and a sweeping front porch, exuding timeless charm and grace.
The urban neighborhood was characterized by its colorful row houses, each with a unique facade adorned with decorative trim and window boxes.

senses.definition: a storage area or room used for storing ammunition, explosives, or other hazardous materials in a safe and secure manner
a cupboard or small room, often next to kitchen, used for keeping food in

examples.x: She stocked the pantry with canned goods and dried pasta.
The pantry was filled with shelves of spices and baking supplies.
He found the extra cans of beans in the pantry.
They organized the pantry to make it easier to find ingredients.
The family decided to turn the small closet into a pantry for more storage.

senses.definition: a designated space within a building used for storing various items or belongings in an organized manner, typically equipped with shelves, cabinets, or other storage solutions

examples.x: The storage room is full of old boxes that haven’t been opened in years.
We keep the extra cleaning supplies in the storage room at the back of the house.
She uses the storage room to store holiday decorations when they're not in use.
The garage has been converted into a storage room to hold all the tools and equipment.
After the move, we still had a lot of items to organize in the storage room.

senses.definition: an upright structure, usually made of brick, concrete, or stone that is made to divide, protect, or surround a place

examples.x: He noticed a spider crawling up the wall.
He placed a bookshelf against the wall to store his books.
He stands on a ladder to reach the top of the wall for painting.
She hangs a painting on the wall to add color to the room.
She placed a calendar on the wall to keep track of important dates.

senses.definition: the structure that creates the outer top part of a vehicle, building, etc.

examples.x: He placed solar panels on the roof to generate electricity.
She climbed onto the roof to enjoy the view from above.
She noticed a small bird nesting on the roof of her garage.
She sat on the roof of her car to enjoy a picnic under the stars.
The snow on the roof started to melt in the warmth of the sun.

senses.definition: the highest part of a room, vehicle, etc. that covers it from the inside

examples.x: He stood on a ladder to change a lightbulb on the ceiling.
He installed soundproofing material on the ceiling to reduce noise from the floor above.
He noticed a water stain on the ceiling and called a professional to fix the leak.
He used a long brush to paint the high ceiling of the living room.
She lies on the floor, imagining shapes on the ceiling.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment such as a bath that is permanently affixed inside a house or building and people cannot take it out when they move out

examples.x: The bathroom’s new sink is a fixture that won't be removed when we move.
Replacing old fixtures, such as the bathtub and shower, can significantly update a bathroom.
The landlord was responsible for repairing any broken fixtures in the rental property.
The built-in bookcases are considered fixtures and are included in the home sale.
The tenants asked if they could replace the outdated fixtures with modern ones.

senses.definition: the front exterior of a house, apartment, or another building that faces a street or river

examples.x: The frontage of the mansion was adorned with large windows and ornate balconies.
The ivy-covered frontage of the old library gave it a timeless, rustic charm.
The restaurant’s frontage was renovated to attract more customers.

senses.definition: a device used to measure and monitor the flow and consumption of natural gas or propane in residential, commercial, or industrial settings

examples.x: The gas meter outside the house is read by the utility company every month to track our usage.
The technician came to inspect the gas meter and make sure it was working properly.
She checked the gas meter to see if there was any change in the reading after using the stove all evening.
To avoid overpaying, it's important to monitor the gas meter regularly to ensure accuracy.
When moving into a new apartment, the gas meter reading is recorded to establish the starting point for billing.

senses.definition: a space in a building with walls, a floor, and a ceiling where people do different activities

examples.x: I have a big room with a window.
My favorite room in the house is the kitchen because I love cooking.
There is a small room next to the living room for reading.
Please keep your room clean and organized.
We painted my room blue to make it feel more relaxing.
I found a quiet room to study for my exams.

senses.definition: a service that is provided to the public, such as electricity, water, or gas, which is used in daily life

examples.x: In winter, the utility costs rise because of the increased use of heating.
Our utility bill includes charges for waste disposal along with water and electricity.
The government is responsible for ensuring the provision of essential utilities to all citizens.
The storm caused a disruption in utility services throughout the city.
The utility company came to fix the power outage in our neighborhood.

senses.definition: a building later added to a main building in order to provide more space

examples.x: The school built an annex to accommodate the growing number of students.
The hospital’s new annex includes additional patient rooms and surgical suites.
They converted the old annex into a modern office space.
The museum’s annex houses a collection of contemporary art.
The company uses the annex as a training center for new employees.

senses.definition: a channel or passage that lets the smoke from a fire pass through and get out from the roof of a building

examples.x: Smoke rose from the chimney on the cold winter morning.
The bird built a nest inside the chimney.
Santa Claus is often imagined coming down the chimney.
The chimney was cleaned before starting the fireplace.
He saw the flames through the chimney’s opening.

senses.definition: a hood-like covering or structure, often with a conical or curved shape, used to cover or protect an opening or feature such as a chimney, ventilator, or roof
a protective covering made of metal or glass that is placed in front of a fireplace to prevent sparks, embers, or debris from escaping while still allowing heat and light to pass through

examples.x: She carefully adjusted the fire screen to keep the embers contained while the fire burned down.
After the fire had burned out, he removed the fire screen to clean up the ashes.
We decided to get a fire screen with a mesh design to allow better airflow while still being safe.
The fire screen was placed in front of the fireplace to prevent any sparks from flying out.
The ornate metal fire screen added a touch of elegance to the living room.

senses.definition: a shelf that is made of wood or stone and is above a fireplace

examples.x: The family portrait hung proudly above the mantelpiece, capturing cherished memories for generations to come.
She decorated the mantelpiece with a collection of vintage candlesticks and fresh flowers, creating a focal point in the living room.
The antique clock was displayed prominently on the mantelpiece, its intricate design adding character to the room.
The stockings were hung from the mantelpiece with care, ready to be filled with goodies on Christmas morning.
He sat by the fireplace, his gaze fixed on the photograph resting on the mantelpiece, lost in memories of days gone by.

senses.definition: a small and cozy corner or space, usually next to a fireplace, where people can sit and relax in a warm and comfortable environment

examples.x: The family gathered in the inglenook by the fire, enjoying hot cocoa and stories.
In the old cottage, the inglenook was the perfect place to relax on a chilly evening.
They built a cozy inglenook with a stone bench where they could sit and enjoy the warmth of the fireplace.
During the winter months, the inglenook was always the most popular spot in the house.
After a long day, they would retreat to the inglenook to unwind by the fire and chat.

senses.definition: the bottom of a room that we walk on

examples.x: He swept the floor to remove the dust and dirt.
My child sits on the floor to play with his toys.
She accidentally dropped a plate, and it shattered into pieces on the floor.
She covered the floor with a colorful carpet.
She spilled juice on the floor and immediately cleaned it up.

senses.definition: one of the many floors that are in a building

examples.x: The children's department is on the third level of the library.
The elevator goes up to the fifth level where my office is.
The meeting room is on the same level as the reception desk.
The restaurant is on the top level of the building.

senses.definition: a floor situated between two other floors of a building, which is smaller compared to the two

examples.x: The bookstore had a cozy reading area on the mezzanine, offering a quiet retreat for book lovers.
The theater's mezzanine provided excellent views of the stage, with comfortable seats and ample legroom.
The restaurant's mezzanine overlooked the bustling main dining area below, offering diners a more intimate setting.
The hotel's fitness center was located on the mezzanine, allowing guests to stay active while enjoying panoramic views of the city.

senses.definition: a vertical structural element, often made of stone, that supports the weight of the building above it

examples.x: The ancient Greek temples are known for their majestic columns.
The Roman architecture featured grand columns supporting large arches.
The interior of the cathedral was adorned with ornate columns carved from marble.
The architect designed the modern office building with sleek, minimalist columns.
The museum's entrance was framed by towering columns, adding to its grandeur.

senses.definition: the front face of a large building, particularly a church

examples.x: The intricate carvings on the front of the cathedral attract tourists from all over the world.
The front of the historic courthouse was adorned with tall columns and an imposing pediment.
The church's front was adorned with intricate carvings.
They gathered outside the front of the building for the ceremony.
The front of the cathedral featured tall, stained-glass windows.

senses.definition: the area just inside the entrance of a public building such as a hotel, etc.

examples.x: Guests gathered in the hotel lobby, waiting for the shuttle to the airport.
The spacious lobby of the theater buzzed with excitement before the show.
She sat in the lobby, sipping her coffee and watching people come and go.
The lobby of the office building featured modern art installations and comfortable seating areas.
The hotel's grand lobby was adorned with marble floors and chandeliers.

senses.definition: a vertical or inclined passage in a building that allows for the convenient disposal of waste materials by dropping them into a designated collection area or dumpster
a recessed part of a wall that is built further back from the rest of it

examples.x: The library had a cozy alcove with built-in bookshelves, perfect for curling up with a good book.
The restaurant's intimate alcove was tucked away in a corner, offering diners a private dining experience.
The bedroom featured a window alcove, providing a sunny spot for a reading nook or small seating area.
The art gallery had a special alcove dedicated to showcasing sculptures, illuminated by soft overhead lighting.

senses.definition: an opening or passage that allows the movement of air, gases, or other substances, typically used for ventilation, release of heat or moisture, or to remove unwanted odors
an area with no roof that is partially or completely surrounded by walls, often forming a part of a large building

examples.x: The children played in the courtyard, enjoying the fresh air and sunshine.
The hotel featured a beautiful courtyard with a fountain at its center.
After the renovation, the old mansion now has a peaceful courtyard with blooming flowers.
They enjoyed their morning coffee in the courtyard, surrounded by greenery and chirping birds.
The restaurant had an outdoor courtyard where diners could eat under the stars.

senses.definition: a curved architectural space where sound waves can travel along the walls, allowing whispers to be heard clearly from one side to the other

examples.x: The cathedral had a whispering gallery, where visitors could whisper on one side and hear the sound clearly on the other.
The tour guide explained how the whispering gallery in the dome allowed sound to travel across the space with ease.
As she stood in the whispering gallery, Emma was amazed at how her soft voice could be heard on the other side of the room.
The acoustics in the old church were so precise that the whispering gallery allowed even the faintest sounds to be carried across the building.
Standing in the whispering gallery of the grand hall, we could hear each other’s whispers despite being far apart.

senses.definition: a short and wide pipe connected to the top of a chimney

examples.x: The chimney pot on the roof helped direct smoke safely away from the house.
The old cottage had a worn chimney pot that needed replacing after many years of use.
He climbed up to fix the cracked chimney pot before winter set in.
The chimney pot added a decorative touch to the otherwise simple roofline.
After the storm, the chimney pot was knocked off, causing smoke to back up into the house.

senses.definition: a small open space or courtyard enclosed by buildings or walls
a series of rooms, particularly in a hotel

examples.x: The newlyweds stayed in a luxurious honeymoon suite with a stunning ocean view.
The business executive booked a suite to have extra space for meetings and relaxation.
Our suite included a spacious living area, a kitchen, and a private balcony.
The hotel offered complimentary breakfast and concierge services for all guests staying in a suite.
They upgraded to a suite for their anniversary trip to enjoy the added comfort and amenities.

senses.definition: a four-sided courtyard or open space, often enclosed by buildings or walls, typically found in educational institutions, residential complexes, or historical landmarks
a set of steps along with their surrounding structure that can lead one from one floor to another

examples.x: The grand stairway in the mansion was made of marble.
She climbed the narrow stairway to the attic.
The emergency stairway is located at the back of the building.
He paused halfway up the stairway to catch his breath.
They installed lights along the stairway for safety.

senses.definition: a small elevator or lifting device used to transport items such as food, dishes, or laundry between different floors of a building, typically in a commercial or residential setting
a box-like device that moves up and down and is used to get to the different levels of a building

examples.x: I felt a bit nervous when the elevator suddenly stopped between floors.
I prefer taking the elevator in tall buildings rather than using the stairs.
I pressed the button and waited for the elevator to arrive.
It's important to hold the elevator door open if someone is rushing to catch it.
We took the elevator to the top floor of the building.

senses.definition: a staircase that moves and takes people up or down different levels easily, often found in large buildings like airports, department stores, etc.

examples.x: The weary travelers rode the escalator down to the baggage claim area after a long flight.
The department store installed a new escalator to improve accessibility between floors for shoppers.
She hurriedly descended the escalator, anxious to catch her train before it departed.
The airport terminal was bustling with passengers riding escalators to reach their departure gates.
He stood patiently on the escalator, enjoying the leisurely ascent to the top floor of the shopping mall.

senses.definition: a fireplace built into a wall where fires can be lit

examples.x: Grandma knitted by the hearth while Grandpa read aloud.
Guests often gravitate towards the hearth during gatherings.
The crackling fire in the hearth warmed the room.
The cat curled up next to the hearth to stay cozy.
The aroma of wood smoke filled the air around the hearth.

senses.definition: the area or space near a fire, particularly a fireplace, where people gather for warmth, relaxation, and socializing
a long and narrow covered walkway or corridor with a series of arches or columns along one or both sides
a vertical transportation system consisting of a cylindrical tube or shaft in which a cabin or platform travels, enabling efficient movement between different floors or levels within a building
the network of components, wiring, and devices that enable the generation, distribution, and use of electrical power within a building or structure
a network of pipes, fixtures, valves, and other components that work together to supply and distribute water, as well as remove waste and sewage, within a building or structure

examples.x: The plumber inspected the plumbing system to ensure there were no leaks in the pipes.
A well-maintained plumbing system is essential for clean water and efficient waste removal.
Installing an energy-efficient plumbing system can help save water and reduce bills.
The new house featured a modern plumbing system with advanced water-saving features.

senses.definition: the underside of an architectural element, such as a ceiling, arch, or overhanging roof, typically located on the underside of a structure

examples.x: The soffit under the roofline was painted white to match the exterior walls.
Proper ventilation through the soffit helps prevent moisture buildup in the attic.
Birds often try to nest in gaps in the soffit if it is not sealed properly.
The contractor inspected the soffit to ensure it was secure and free from rot.
Installing vents in the soffit improved air circulation throughout the house.

senses.definition: a level or floor of a building or structure, typically with a horizontal division that separates it from other levels, often used to define the height or number of floors in a building
an upper floor of a house, apartment, or any other building

examples.x: She moved the furniture to the upstairs for better organization.
The upstairs of the house has two bedrooms and a small study.
They renovated the upstairs to add a new bathroom.
The children love playing in the upstairs where there’s more space.

senses.definition: a floor that is located on a lower level of a building, particularly at the ground level

examples.x: The downstairs of the house is newly renovated.
The downstairs area has a spacious kitchen and dining room.
We moved all the furniture to the downstairs for the party.

senses.definition: the internal part of a building, car, etc.

examples.x: The house’s interior features original woodwork and high ceilings.
After years of neglect, the church’s interior was fully restored.
The interior of the museum is filled with beautiful artwork and sculptures.
The interior of the house was decorated in a modern style.
They cleaned the interior of the house before the guests arrived.

senses.definition: the outer surface or outermost layer of an object, building, etc.

examples.x: The mug’s glossy exterior was decorated with colorful patterns.
Dirt and scratches had accumulated on the car’s exterior over time.
She wiped down the box’s exterior before opening it.
The vase’s delicate exterior was painted with intricate floral designs.
The building’s stone exterior gave it a timeless, elegant look.
